text: Dirtus is sitting on the earth inside the Scathagram, very still. He's not crying. He's just very present in a way he isn't usually.

speaker: Dirtus
text: Like I've been working toward something for a long time. And I don't know if it's done or if it's just starting.

speaker: Grubwin
text: That's — a very reasonable thing to feel.

speaker: Mawla
text: For what it's worth. Whatever that was — it happened because all five of us showed up. Wrote things. Meant them.

speaker: Mawla
text: That's not nothing. That's not even a little nothing.

speaker: Vermia
text: Mawla has been saying the most profound things lately.
The water is still rising slowly in the channels. The worms are still there. The moon is overhead. Nobody moves for a while.
And then Grubwin, without any announcement, starts humming the Grand Hymn.
One by one, everyone joins. In the old greenhouse, in the moonlight, surrounded by worms that came up through wet earth.
This is, whatever else it is, exactly enough.
